market_summary: US equity futures are pointing to a lower open as a sharp rise in oil prices above $95/bbl and escalating US-Iran tensions weigh on risk appetite. The market is in a holding pattern ahead of after-close earnings from Alphabet and Tesla, which will set the tone for the AI and mega-cap growth narrative. Overnight, Super Micro Computer surged on strong margins and orders, providing a positive counterweight in the tech hardware space, while energy stocks are set to rally on the oil move.
macro_summary: Geopolitical Shock in a Resilient Earnings Environment The market is in a 'wait and see' mode, caught between a positive earnings backdrop and a negative macro shock from rising oil prices. The next move will be dictated by the outcome of Big Tech earnings and any de-escalation in the US-Iran conflict. Today's market is characterized by a sudden and sharp increase in geopolitical risk that is testing the resilience of a fundamentally sound earnings season. The oil spike is the primary driver, forcing a reassessment of inflation and growth expectations, while the market waits for Big Tech results to provide direction. The macro outlook is dominated by a sudden geopolitical shock that is complicating the inflation outlook. While the Fed remains data-dependent, a sustained move above $95 oil could rekindle inflation fears and delay rate cuts. The market is now pricing in a higher probability of a 'stagflationary' scenario, where growth slows but inflation remains sticky.
risk_summary: Risk appetite is clearly waning as the VIX is expected to open higher. Breadth is likely to be negative, with defensive sectors like utilities and consumer staples seeing relative strength. The oil spike is a clear negative for consumer discretionary and transport stocks, while energy is the only clear winner. The market is not in panic mode, but the 'risk-on' trade is on hold until the geopolitical situation clarifies and Big Tech earnings provide a new catalyst.

top_events.title: Oil Surges Above $95 on US-Iran Escalation
top_events.summary: Oil prices climbed to a six-week high above $95/bbl after President Trump threatened to bomb Iran's bridges and power plants if it attacks ships in the Strait of Hormuz. The US has struck Iranian targets for an 11th straight night.

top_events.title: Trump vows to bomb Iran's bridges, power plants if it shoots at ships in Hormuz Strait
top_events.summary: He issued his threat after U.S. Central Command hit Iranian targets for an 11th straight night.

top_events.title: Oil prices rise another 3%, while Wall Street drifts in mixed trading
top_events.summary: Oil prices rise another 3%, while Wall Street drifts in mixed trading AP News

top_events.title: Super Micro Computer (SMCI) Surges 15% on Strong Margins and SpaceX Order
top_events.summary: SMCI shares surged 15% in pre-market after the company disclosed booming margins and a new order from SpaceX. The news also lifted Dell and HP Enterprise, signaling strong demand for AI servers.

top_events.title: Treasury yields hit two-month high on oil surge, inflation fears
top_events.summary: Treasury yields hit two-month high on oil surge, inflation fears Investing.com Canada

top_events.title: AMD to Invest Up to $5 Billion in Anthropic
top_events.summary: AMD announced a deal to invest up to $5 billion in AI startup Anthropic as part of a computing power agreement. This is the latest in a string of infrastructure deals for Anthropic.

top_events.title: Trump's push for American-made AI chips hits TSMC's margins
top_events.summary: The Taiwan-based chipmaker has announced $200 billion in investment into U.S. manufacturing since Trump returned to power in 2025.

top_events.title: Apple Plans Overhaul of MacBooks, iMac in Push to Meet AI Demand
top_events.summary: Apple Plans Overhaul of MacBooks, iMac in Push to Meet AI Demand Bloomberg.com

sector_summary: Positive sectors/themes: Energy (XLE), Oil & Gas Equipment & Services (OIH), Defense (ITA); Negative sectors/themes: Airlines (JETS), Consumer Discretionary (XLY), Transportation (IYT), Homebuilders (XHB); Themes to watch: Geopolitical Risk Premium, AI Infrastructure Buildout, Consumer Resilience vs. Energy Shock
